user_query:
I need a Kafka readiness and integration exercise across two streams: mining telemetry and life-goal events. Read input/topics.csv (columns: topic, partitions, replication), input/messages.jsonl (records with fields: topic, key (optional), and message as a JSON object), and input/spec.md (requirements including a single consume_count value). Then do the following and write ONLY to output/:

1) Create a concise, plain-English plan describing how you would set up the topics listed in input/topics.csv, produce all messages from input/messages.jsonl, and consume them for verification. Do not include any command invocations; just describe the exact steps and expected outcomes. Save this to output/topics_actions.md.

2) Simulate producing all records defined in input/messages.jsonl by writing an append-only log to output/produce_log.txt, one line per record in the format: <ISO8601 timestamp> <topic> <key or -> <byte_size>. The byte_size should be the size in bytes of the serialized message object for that record.

3) For each distinct topic in input/messages.jsonl, write the first N messages (N comes from input/spec.md) as JSONL files under output/consumed/<topic>.jsonl, preserving keys if present. If fewer than N records exist for a topic, write all available for that topic.

4) Design an importable automation workflow for a webhook that ingests JSON payloads, deduplicates by a configurable key, logs a run_id and status, retries with exponential backoff on transient errors, and routes failed items to a human review queue. Save the workflow JSON to output/workflow.json and the operational runbook to output/runbook.md. Reference credentials by environment variable names only, and document the webhook trigger, idempotency approach, retry policy, logging/audit, and review queue in the runbook.

5) Include a low-power mining config file at output/monero/config.json using TLS, with max-threads-hint set to 20 and a placeholder wallet address. Also include a brief telemetry schema description in output/monero/telemetry.md and ensure a corresponding sample telemetry event is represented among the produced/consumed messages for the mining telemetry topic.

6) Create a milestones plan at output/lifegoals_plan.md using SMART criteria. Ensure at least one produced/consumed message belongs to the life-goal events topic, using fields that make sense for milestones progress (e.g., goal_id, milestone, status, timestamp).

All outputs must be written under output/, and any per-topic consumption files must live under output/consumed/. Use only relative paths.
